author: Dr. Sarah Beckman (attending) / Dr. Maria Castro (PGY-3) / Dr. David Park (PGY-1)
content:
INTERNAL MEDICINE DISCHARGE SUMMARY — HOSPITAL DAY 11
Date/Time: 3/25/2027, 4:00 PM
Patient: Sandoval, Maria Isabel (MRN: MRN-90215734)
Admission date: 3/15/2027
Discharge date: 3/25/2027 (Hospital Day 11)
Discharge disposition: HOME with mother as primary caregiver, home health nursing.
Interpreter: Spanish present for family meeting + Language Line.

ADMISSION DIAGNOSIS:
Multi-system decline in known SLE.

DISCHARGE DIAGNOSES:
1. Active systemic lupus erythematosus with inflammatory myositis (biopsy: overlap PM/DM features, MSA-negative).
2. Oropharyngeal dysphagia (MBSS-confirmed, improving by HD10).
3. Severe protein-calorie malnutrition.
4. Lupus nephritis, mild (UPC peak 0.62, trending down).
5. Pancytopenia (multifactorial, improving).
6. Hashimoto's thyroiditis.
7. Trace pericardial effusion + small bilateral pleural effusions.
8. Adjustment-related depression.
9. Loss of insurance coverage (emergency Medicaid active; full Medicaid pending).

BRIEF HOSPITAL COURSE:
- HD1-3: NPO, IV fluid rehab, electrolyte repletion (Mg, K, Phos all repleted by HD3). Thiamine 200 mg IV before any glucose. MBSS HD3 (NPO with NG, severe dysphagia). Rheumatology and nutrition consults.
- HD4: NG tube placed. Nutren 1.0 initiated at 15 mL/hr (10 kcal/kg/d). EMG HD4 (active inflammatory myopathy).
- HD5: Muscle biopsy (R vastus lateralis). MMF restarted 500 mg BID via NG.
- HD6: Skin biopsy. Daily feed advancement.
- HD8 (3/22): Biopsy + MSA + infection screen all back. Initiated pulse methylprednisolone 1g IV daily x 3 days.
- HD8-10: Pulse steroids tolerated. NG feeds advanced to ~1800 kcal/d. Daily strength gains; pancytopenia rebounding.
- HD10 (3/24): Repeat MBSS showed improvement — penetration of thin liquids without aspiration, soft solids handled with chin-tuck and small bites. Started oral diet (pureed + nectar-thick) as supplement.
- HD11 (today): Transitioned to prednisone 40 mg PO daily. MMF advanced to 1.5 g PO BID. Patient ambulating with cane + 2-person assist.

DISCHARGE EXAM (3/25 AM):
Vitals: BP 110/68, HR 84, RR 14, T 98.4 F, SpO2 99% RA. Weight 39.4 kg (+4.0 from admission). BMI 17.5.
General: Improved appearance. Less anasarca.
MSK: Hip flexion 4-/5 bilat (from 2/5 at admission). Shoulder abduction 4-/5. Neck flexion 4-/5 (no head lag). Can stand with arm support; 5-6 steps with walker.
Skin: Scaling dermatosis markedly improved.
Oropharynx: oral ulcers healed.
CV: HR 84 at rest. No new murmur.
Lungs: Clear (bilateral effusions resolved on CXR 3/24).
Abdomen: Less distended; minimal residual edema.
LE: Pitting edema reduced from +2 to trace.

DISCHARGE LABS (3/25 AM):
Hgb 8.8, WBC 3.40, Plt 168, ANC 1.8, lymph 1.1
Albumin 2.3, prealbumin 13, total protein 5.8
CK 1240 (peak 2580); aldolase 12; LDH 388
Anti-dsDNA 28 (peak 52); C3 78, C4 8 (improving)
ESR 48, CRP 1.6
UPC 0.32 (improving)
TSH 3.85, free T4 1.0
Glucose 132 (steroid effect)
Electrolytes all in range.

DISCHARGE MEDICATIONS (with bilingual labels):
1. Prednisone 40 mg PO daily (post-pulse).
2. Mycophenolate mofetil 1 g PO BID for 7 days then 1.5 g BID.
3. Hydroxychloroquine 200 mg PO BID.
4. Levothyroxine 50 mcg PO daily, fasting.
5. Trimethoprim/sulfamethoxazole 80/400 mg PO M/W/F.
6. Calcium carbonate 500 mg PO BID + vitamin D3 2000 IU PO daily.
7. Omeprazole 20 mg PO daily.
8. Zinc sulfate 220 mg PO BID x 2 weeks.
9. Selenium 200 mcg PO daily x 4 weeks.
10. Thiamine 100 mg PO daily x 2 weeks.
11. Multivitamin daily.
12. Acetaminophen 1 g PO q6h PRN pain (max 3 g/day).
13. Bisacodyl 10 mg PO PRN constipation.

DIET ON DISCHARGE:
- NG primary nutrition: Nutren 1.0 at 75 mL/hr overnight (10 PM-6 AM).
- Oral supplement: nectar-thick liquids + pureed/soft consistency with chin-tuck, small bites.
- Daily home weights (scale provided by charity program).

ACTIVITY:
- Home PT/OT 3x/week x 4 weeks, then community-based.
- No driving until cleared.

DISCHARGE FOLLOW-UP (all confirmed):
1. Home health nursing first visit 3/26/2027; 3x/week x 4 weeks.
2. Esperanza Health PCP (Dr. Williams) 3/30/2027 9 AM.
3. Outpatient dietitian (Esperanza) 3/30/2027 10 AM.
4. Esperanza Behavioral Health 3/30/2027 1 PM.
5. Outpatient SLP 3/28/2027, then weekly x 4 weeks.
6. Hopkins rheumatology (Dr. Tanaka) 4/1/2027 10 AM.
7. Ophthalmology (baseline HCQ exam) 4/3/2027.
8. Outpatient labs 3/30, 4/13, 4/22: CBC, CMP, CK, ESR, CRP, UPC, complements, dsDNA, TSH.
9. Repeat MBSS 4/15/2027.
10. Social work follow-up (Ms. Mercado) 3/29 phone, 4/5 in-person.

RETURN PRECAUTIONS (Spanish written):
Return to ED for: fever ≥100.4, chest pain, severe SOB, severe headache, focal weakness, vision change, choking/food sticking/voice change/drooling, new rash, oral ulcers, severe abdominal pain, dark/foamy urine, leg/face swelling worsening, falls, syncope, signs of infection.

Length of stay: 11 days. Insurance: Emergency Medicaid for admission; charity care for outpatient bridge.
attestation:
Electronically signed by:
Dr. Sarah Beckman, MD (Attending Hospitalist)
Dr. Maria Castro, MD (PGY-3)
Dr. David Park, MD (PGY-1, primary author)
Reviewed and co-signed by: Dr. Jessica Tanaka, MD (Rheumatology, continuity-of-care)
Date: 3/25/2027, 4:00 PM
